title: New Jersey Turnpike Authority v. Devon T. Barber
court: Superior Court of New Jersey, Special Civil Part,
parties: New Jersey Turnpike Authority v. Devon T. Barber
summary: Special Civil Part toll enforcement action involving statutory authority, administrative fee structure, and procedural compliance. The matter includes responsive pleadings and discovery activity.
sourceNotes: Documents obtained via public records requests and court filings.

timeline.title: Complaint and Summons Filed
timeline.description: NJTA files toll enforcement complaint in Special Civil Part.

timeline.title: Answer and First Appearance
timeline.description: Devon Barber files answer and first appearance pro se.

timeline.title: Motion to Dismiss Filed
timeline.description: Defendant files motion to dismiss the complaint.

timeline.title: NJTA Opposition to Motion to Dismiss
timeline.description: NJTA files opposition to defendant's motion to dismiss.

timeline.title: Counterclaim and Default Judgment Request
timeline.description: Defendant files counterclaim, proposed orders, and request to enter default judgment.

timeline.title: Court Order and Discovery
timeline.description: Court order entered. Discovery interrogatories and requests served.

timeline.title: NJTA Answers Counterclaim
timeline.description: NJTA files answer to counterclaim with affirmative defenses.

timeline.title: Motions to Quash Discovery
timeline.description: Both sides file competing motions to quash discovery requests.

timeline.title: Discovery Dispute Briefing
timeline.description: Motions to compel, objections, and bench summary filed.

timeline.title: Order Granted in Error
timeline.description: Court enters order later identified as erroneous. Correspondence on the error follows.

timeline.title: Class Action and Mediation Motion
timeline.description: Defendant files motion proposing class action service and memorialization of mediation. Additional motions and opposition filed.

overview: Special Civil Part toll enforcement action filed by the New Jersey Turnpike Authority against Devon T. Barber. The case involves statutory authority challenges, administrative fee disputes, and procedural compliance questions. Defendant has filed a counterclaim and motion to dismiss. Active discovery disputes are ongoing with competing motions to quash and compel. ADA accommodation requests have been made. A motion proposing class action service and mediation has been filed.

reviewNotes.damagesInjuries: Toll enforcement dispute. NJTA claims unpaid tolls and administrative fees. Defendant challenges statutory authority, fee structure legality, and procedural compliance. Counterclaim filed seeking relief.
reviewNotes.keyEvidenceSources: NJTA toll records, complaint and summons filings, defendant's answer and counterclaim, court orders, discovery responses, ADA correspondence, NJ Constitution provisions.
reviewNotes.deadlinesLimitations: Special Civil Part matter. Discovery ongoing with competing motions to quash and compel. Scheduling notices issued for pending motions. ADA accommodations requested.
reviewNotes.reliefSought: Defendant seeks dismissal of complaint, entry of default judgment on counterclaim, fee waiver, class action certification, and mediation. NJTA seeks toll enforcement and administrative fees.
reviewNotes.notes: Pro se defendant with active counterclaim. Multiple procedural challenges filed. ADA accommodation requests on record. Discovery disputes reveal competing positions on document production. Court has entered at least one order identified as erroneous.

contingencyChecklist.label: Class action and mediation proposal filed
Discovery disputes resolved
contingencyChecklist.notes: Competing motions to quash and compel still pending

contingencyChecklist.label: Erroneous order corrected
contingencyChecklist.notes: Order granted in error 2025-12-15, correspondence sent, correction pending
